   199→- `ttl`: Time-to-live in milliseconds (default: 1 hour)
   200→
   201→#### Graceful Degradation (AC-6)
   202→
   203→If the cache is corrupted or unreadable:
   204→1. Log warning at debug level (AC-7)
   205→2. Fall back to fresh run
   206→3. Continue without caching errors affecting QA
   207→
   208→### Pre-flight Sync Check
   209→
   210→**Skip this section if `SEQUANT_ORCHESTRATOR` is set** - the orchestrator has already verified sync status.
   211→
   212→Before starting QA (standalone mode), verify the local branch is in sync with remote:
   213→
   214→```bash
   215→git fetch origin 2>/dev/null || echo "Network unavailable - proceeding with local state"
   216→git status -sb | head -1  # Shows ahead/behind status
   217→```
   218→
   219→**Status interpretation:**
   220→- `[ahead N]` - Local has commits not on remote (OK to proceed)
   221→- `[behind N]` - Remote has commits not pulled locally (recommend sync first)
   222→- `[ahead N, behind M]` - Branches diverged (recommend sync before QA)
   223→
   224→If diverged, recommend:
   225→```bash
   226→git pull origin main  # Or merge origin/main if pull fails
   227→```
   228→
   229→### Stale Branch Detection
   230→
   231→**Skip this section if `SEQUANT_ORCHESTRATOR` is set** - the orchestrator handles branch freshness checks.
   232→
   233→**Purpose:** Detect when the feature branch is significantly behind main, which can lead to:
   234→- QA cycles wasted reviewing code that won't cleanly merge
   235→- False `READY_FOR_MERGE` verdicts that fail at merge time
   236→- Conflicts that require rework after QA approval
   237→
   238→**Detection:**
   239→
   240→```bash
   241→# Ensure we have latest remote state
   242→git fetch origin 2>/dev/null || true
   243→
   244→# Count commits behind main
   245→behind=$(git rev-list --count HEAD..origin/main 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
   246→echo "Feature branch is $behind commits behind main"
   247→```
   248→
   249→**Threshold Configuration:**
   250→
   251→The stale branch threshold is configurable in `.sequant/settings.json`:
   252→
   253→```json
   254→{
   255→  "run": {
   256→    "staleBranchThreshold": 5
   257→  }
   258→}
   259→```
   260→
   261→Default: 5 commits
   262→
   263→**Behavior:**
   264→
   265→| Commits Behind | Action |
   266→|----------------|--------|
   267→| 0 | ✅ Proceed normally |
   268→| 1 to threshold | ⚠️ **Warning:** "Feature branch is N commits behind main. Consider rebasing before QA." |
   269→| > threshold | ❌ **Block:** "STALE_BRANCH: Feature branch is N commits behind main (threshold: T). Rebase required before QA." |
   270→
   271→**Implementation:**
   272→
   273→```bash
   274→# Read threshold from settings (default: 5)
   275→threshold=$(jq -r '.run.staleBranchThreshold // 5' .sequant/settings.json 2>/dev/null || echo "5")
   276→
   277→behind=$(git rev-list --count HEAD..origin/main 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
   278→
   279→if [[ $behind -gt $threshold ]]; then
   280→  echo "❌ STALE_BRANCH: Feature branch is $behind commits behind main (threshold: $threshold)"
   281→  echo "   Rebase required before QA:"
   282→  echo "   git fetch origin && git rebase origin/main"
   283→  # Exit with error - QA should not proceed
   284→  exit 1
   285→elif [[ $behind -gt 0 ]]; then
   286→  echo "⚠️ Warning: Feature branch is $behind commits behind main."
   287→  echo "   Consider rebasing before QA: git fetch origin && git rebase origin/main"
   288→  # Continue with warning
   289→fi
   290→```
   291→
   292→**Output Format:**
   293→
   294→Include in QA output when branch is stale:
   295→
   296→```markdown
   297→### Stale Branch Check
   298→
   299→| Check | Value |
   300→|-------|-------|
   301→| Commits behind main | N |
   302→| Threshold | T |
   303→| Status | ✅ OK / ⚠️ Warning / ❌ Blocked |
   304→
   305→[Warning/blocking message if applicable]
   306→```
   307→
   308→**Verdict Impact:**
   309→
   310→| Status | Verdict Impact |
   311→|--------|----------------|
   312→| OK (0 behind) | No impact |
   313→| Warning (1 to threshold) | Note in findings, recommend rebase |
   314→| Blocked (> threshold) | **Cannot proceed** - rebase first |
   315→
   316→### Feature Worktree Workflow
   317→
   318→**QA Phase:** Review code in the feature worktree.
   319→
   320→**If orchestrated (SEQUANT_WORKTREE is set):**
   321→- Use the provided worktree path directly: `cd $SEQUANT_WORKTREE`
   322→- Skip step 1 below (worktree location provided by orchestrator)
   323→
   324→**If standalone:**
   325→
   326→1. **Locate the worktree:**
   327→   - The worktree should already exist from the execution phase (`/exec`)
   328→   - Find the worktree: `git worktree list` or check `../worktrees/feature/` for directories matching the issue number
   329→   - The worktree path will be: `../worktrees/feature/<issue-number>-<issue-title-slug>/`
   330→
   331→2. **Check implementation status:**
   332→   - Navigate to worktree: `cd <worktree-path>`
   333→   - Check for uncommitted changes: `git status`
   334→   - Check for committed changes: `git log --oneline main..HEAD`
   335→
   336→   **Status interpretation:**
   337→   - **No commits AND no uncommitted changes:** Implementation may not be started
   338→   - **Uncommitted changes exist:** Implementation done but not committed
   339→   - **Commits exist:** Implementation committed and ready for review
   340→
   341→3. **Review in the worktree:**
   342→   - Navigate to the worktree directory to review the implementation
   343→   - Use `git diff main...HEAD` to see all changes made in the feature branch
   344→   - Run `npm test` and `npm run build` in the worktree to verify everything works
   345→   - Review the code changes against the AC checklist
   346→
   347→4. **Pre-merge cleanup check:**
   348→   - Check for untracked files in main that match PR files: `git status --short`
   349→   - If found, compare versions and remove older local copies
   350→
   351→**Important:** Review the actual implementation in the worktree, not the main branch.
   352→
   353→### No Worktree Found
   354→
   355→If no feature worktree exists (work was done directly on main):
   356→
   357→1. **Identify relevant commits:**
   358→   ```bash
   359→   git log --oneline -10
   360→   ```
   361→
   362→2. **Find the base commit** (before the implementation started):
   363→   ```bash
   364→   # Look for the last commit before the feature work
   365→   git log --oneline --before="<date>" -1
   366→   ```
   367→
   368→3. **Review changes from base:**
   369→   ```bash
   370→   git diff <base-commit>...HEAD --name-only
   371→   git diff <base-commit>...HEAD
   372→   ```
   373→
   374→4. **Run quality checks** on the current branch instead of comparing to a worktree.
   375→
   376→### Phase 0: Implementation Status Check — REQUIRED
   377→
   378→**Before spawning quality check agents**, verify that implementation actually exists. Running full QA on an unimplemented issue wastes tokens and produces confusing output.
   379→
   380→**Detection Logic:**
   381→
   382→```bash
   383→# 1. Check for worktree (indicates work may have started)
   384→worktree_path=$(git worktree list | grep -i "<issue-number>" | awk '{print $1}' | head -1 || true)
   385→
   386→# 2. Check for commits on feature branch (vs main)
   387→commits_exist=$(git log --oneline main..HEAD 2>/dev/null | head -1)
   388→
   389→# 3. Check for uncommitted changes
   390→uncommitted_changes=$(git status --porcelain | head -1)
   391→
   392→# 4. Check for open PR linked to this issue
   393→pr_exists=$(gh pr list --search "<issue-number>" --state open --json number -q '.[0].number' 2>/dev/null)
   394→```
   395→
   396→**Implementation Status Matrix:**
   397→
   398→| Worktree | Commits | Uncommitted | PR | Status | Action |
   399→|----------|---------|-------------|-----|--------|--------|
   400→| ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | No implementation | Early exit |
   401→| ✅ | ❌ | ❌ | ❌ | Worktree created but no work | Early exit |
   402→| ✅ | ❌ | ✅ | ❌ | Work in progress (uncommitted) | Proceed with QA |
   403→| ✅ | ✅ | * | * | Implementation exists | Proceed with QA |
   404→| * | ✅ | * | * | Commits exist | Proceed with QA |
   405→| * | * | * | ✅ | PR exists | Proceed with QA |
   406→
   407→**Early Exit Condition:**
   408→- No commits on feature branch AND no uncommitted changes AND no open PR
   409→
   410→**If early exit triggered:**
   411→1. **Skip** sub-agent spawning (nothing to check)
   412→2. **Skip** code review (no code to review)
   413→3. **Skip** quality metrics collection
   414→4. Use the **Early Exit Output Template** below
   415→5. Verdict: `AC_NOT_MET`
   416→
   417→---
   418→
   419→### Early Exit Output Template
   420→
   421→When no implementation is detected, use this streamlined output:
   422→
   423→```markdown
   424→## QA Review for Issue #<N>
   425→
   426→### Implementation Status: NOT FOUND
   427→
   428→No implementation detected for this issue:
   429→- Commits on feature branch: None
   430→- Uncommitted changes: None
   431→- Open PR: None
   432→
   433→**Verdict: AC_NOT_MET**
   434→
   435→No code changes found to review. The acceptance criteria cannot be evaluated without an implementation.
   436→
   437→### Next Steps
   438→
   439→1. Run `/exec <issue-number>` to implement the feature
   440→2. Re-run `/qa <issue-number>` after implementation is complete
   441→
   442→---
   443→
   444→*QA skipped: No implementation to review*
   445→```
   446→
   447→**Important:** Do NOT spawn sub-agents when using early exit. This saves tokens and avoids confusing "no changes found" outputs from quality checkers.
   448→
   449→---
   450→
   451→### Phase 0b: Quality Plan Verification (CONDITIONAL)
   452→
   453→**When to apply:** If issue has a Feature Quality Planning section in comments (from `/spec`).
   454→
   455→**Purpose:** Verify that quality dimensions identified during planning were addressed in implementation. This catches gaps that AC verification alone misses.
   456→
   457→**Detection:**
   458→```bash
   459→# Check if issue has quality planning section in comments
   460→quality_plan_exists=$(gh issue view <issue> --comments --json comments -q '.comments[].body' | grep -q "Feature Quality Planning" && echo "yes" || echo "no")
   461→```
   462→
   463→**If Quality Plan found:**
   464→
   465→1. **Extract quality dimensions** from the spec comment:
   466→   - Completeness Check items
   467→   - Error Handling items
   468→   - Code Quality items
   469→   - Test Coverage Plan items
   470→   - Best Practices items
   471→   - Polish items (if UI feature)
   472→   - Derived ACs
   473→
   474→2. **Verify each dimension against implementation:**
   475→
   476→   | Dimension | Verification Method |
   477→   |-----------|---------------------|
   478→   | Completeness | Check all AC steps have code |
   479→   | Error Handling | Search for error handling code, try/catch blocks |
   480→   | Code Quality | Check for `any` types, magic strings |
   481→   | Test Coverage | Verify test files exist for critical paths |
   482→   | Best Practices | Check for logging, security patterns |
   483→   | Polish | Check loading/error/empty states in UI |
   484→
   485→3. **Extract and Verify Derived ACs:**
   486→
   487→   **Extraction Method:**
   488→   ```bash
   489→   # Extract derived ACs from spec comment's Derived ACs table
   490→   # Format: | Source | AC-N: Description | Priority |
   491→   # Uses flexible pattern to match any source dimension (not hardcoded)
   492→   derived_acs=$(gh issue view <issue-number> --comments --json comments -q '.comments[].body' | \
   493→     grep -E '\|[^|]+\|\s*AC-[0-9]+:' | \
   494→     grep -oE 'AC-[0-9]+:[^|]+' | \
   495→     sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//;s/[[:space:]]*$//' | \
   496→     sort -u || true)
   497→
   498→   # Count derived ACs
   499→   derived_count=$(echo "$derived_acs" | grep -c "AC-" || echo "0")
   500→   echo "Found $derived_count derived ACs"
   501→   ```
   502→
   503→   **Handling Edge Cases:**
   504→   - **0 derived ACs:** Output "Derived ACs: None found" and skip derived AC verification
   505→   - **1+ derived ACs:** Include each in AC coverage table with source attribution
   506→   - **Malformed rows:** Rows missing the `| Source | AC-N: ... |` pattern are skipped
   507→   - **Extra whitespace:** Trimmed during extraction
   508→
   509→   **Verification:**
   510→   - Treat derived ACs identically to original ACs
   511→   - Include in AC coverage table with "Derived ([Source])" notation
   512→   - Mark as MET/PARTIALLY_MET/NOT_MET based on implementation evidence
   513→
   514→**Output Format:**
   515→
   516→```markdown
   517→### Quality Plan Verification
   518→
   519→**Quality Plan found:** Yes/No
   520→
   521→| Dimension | Items Planned | Items Addressed | Status |
   522→|-----------|---------------|-----------------|--------|
   523→| Completeness | 5 | 5 | ✅ Complete |
   524→| Error Handling | 3 | 2 | ⚠️ Partial (missing: API timeout) |
   525→| Code Quality | 4 | 4 | ✅ Complete |
   526→| Test Coverage | 3 | 3 | ✅ Complete |
   527→| Best Practices | 2 | 2 | ✅ Complete |
   528→| Polish | N/A | N/A | - (not UI feature) |
   529→
   530→**Derived ACs:** 2/2 addressed
   531→
   532→**Quality Plan Status:** Complete / Partial / Not Addressed
   533→```
   534→
   535→**Verdict Impact:**
   536→
   537→| Quality Plan Status | Verdict Impact |
   538→|---------------------|----------------|
   539→| Complete | No impact (positive signal) |
   540→| Partial | Note in findings, consider `AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS` |
   541→| Not Addressed | Flag in findings, may indicate gaps |
   542→| No Plan Found | Note: "Quality plan not available - standard QA only" |
   543→
   544→**Status Threshold Definitions:**
   545→
   546→| Status | Criteria |
   547→|--------|----------|
   548→| **Complete** | All applicable dimensions have ≥80% items addressed |
   549→| **Partial** | At least 50% of applicable dimensions have items addressed |
   550→| **Not Addressed** | <50% of applicable dimensions addressed, or 0 items addressed |
   551→
   552→*Example: If 4 dimensions apply (Completeness, Error Handling, Code Quality, Test Coverage):*
   553→- *Complete: 4/4 dimensions at ≥80%*
   554→- *Partial: 2-3/4 dimensions have work done*
   555→- *Not Addressed: 0-1/4 dimensions have work done*
   556→
   557→**If no Quality Plan found:**
   558→- Output: "Quality Plan Verification: N/A - No quality plan found in issue comments"
   559→- Proceed with standard QA (no verdict impact)
   560→
   561→---
   562→
   563→### Phase 1: CI Status Check — REQUIRED
   564→
   565→**Purpose:** Check GitHub CI status before finalizing verdict. CI-dependent AC items (e.g., "Tests pass in CI") should reflect actual CI status, not just local test results.
   566→
   567→**When to check:** If a PR exists for the issue/branch.
   568→
   569→**Detection:**
   570→```bash
   571→# Get PR number for current branch
   572→pr_number=$(gh pr view --json number -q '.number' 2>/dev/null)
   573→
   574→# If PR exists, check CI status
   575→if [[ -n "$pr_number" ]]; then
   576→  gh pr checks "$pr_number" --json name,state,bucket
   577→fi
   578→```
   579→
   580→**CI Status Mapping:**
   581→
   582→| State | Bucket | AC Status | Verdict Impact |
   583→|-------|--------|-----------|----------------|
   584→| `SUCCESS` | `pass` | `MET` | No impact |
   585→| `FAILURE` | `fail` | `NOT_MET` | Blocks merge |
   586→| `CANCELLED` | `fail` | `NOT_MET` | Blocks merge |
   587→| `SKIPPED` | `pass` | `N/A` | No impact |
   588→| `PENDING` | `pending` | `PENDING` | → `NEEDS_VERIFICATION` |
   589→| `QUEUED` | `pending` | `PENDING` | → `NEEDS_VERIFICATION` |
   590→| `IN_PROGRESS` | `pending` | `PENDING` | → `NEEDS_VERIFICATION` |
   591→| (empty response) | - | `N/A` | No CI configured |
   592→
   593→**CI-Related AC Detection:**
   594→
   595→Identify AC items that depend on CI by matching these patterns:
   596→- "Tests pass in CI"
   597→- "CI passes"
   598→- "Build succeeds in CI"
   599→- "GitHub Actions pass"
   600→- "Pipeline passes"
   601→- "Workflow passes"
   602→- "Checks pass"
   603→- "Actions succeed"
   604→- "CI/CD passes"
   605→
   606→```bash
   607→# Example: Check if any AC mentions CI
   608→ci_ac_patterns="CI|pipeline|GitHub Actions|build succeeds|tests pass in CI|workflow|checks pass|actions succeed"
   609→```
   610→
   611→**Error Handling:**
   612→
   613→If `gh pr checks` fails or returns unexpected results:
   614→- **`gh` not installed** → Skip CI section with note: "CI status unavailable (gh CLI not found)"
   615→- **`gh` not authenticated** → Skip CI section with note: "CI status unavailable (gh auth required)"
   616→- **Network/auth error** → Treat as N/A with note: "CI status unavailable (gh command failed)"
   617→- **No PR exists** → Skip CI status section entirely
   618→- **Empty response** → No CI configured (not an error)
   619→
   620→**Portability Note:**
   621→
   622→CI status detection requires GitHub. Other platforms (GitLab, Bitbucket, Azure DevOps) are not supported. To check if `gh` is available:
   623→```bash
   624→if ! command -v gh &>/dev/null; then
   625→  echo "gh CLI not installed - skipping CI status check"
   626→fi
   627→```
   628→
   629→**Output Format:**
   630→
   631→Include CI status in the QA output:
   632→
   633→```markdown
   634→### CI Status
   635→
   636→| Check | State | Bucket | Impact |
   637→|-------|-------|--------|--------|
   638→| `build (18.x)` | SUCCESS | pass | ✅ MET |
   639→| `build (20.x)` | PENDING | pending | ⏳ PENDING |
   640→| `lint` | FAILURE | fail | ❌ NOT_MET |
   641→
   642→**CI Summary:** 1 passed, 1 pending, 1 failed
   643→**CI-related AC items:** AC-4 ("Tests pass in CI") → PENDING (CI still running)
   644→```
   645→
   646→**No CI Configured:**
   647→
   648→If `gh pr checks` returns an empty response:
   649→```markdown
   650→### CI Status
   651→
   652→No CI checks configured for this repository.
   653→
   654→**CI-related AC items:** AC-4 ("Tests pass in CI") → N/A (no CI configured)
   655→```
   656→
   657→**Verdict Integration:**
   658→
   659→CI status affects the final verdict through the standard verdict algorithm:
   660→- CI `PENDING` → AC item marked `PENDING` → Verdict: `NEEDS_VERIFICATION`
   661→- CI `failure` → AC item marked `NOT_MET` → Verdict: `AC_NOT_MET`
   662→- CI `success` → AC item marked `MET` → No additional impact
   663→- No CI → AC item marked `N/A` → No impact on verdict
   664→
   665→**Important:** Do NOT give `READY_FOR_MERGE` if any CI check is still pending. The correct verdict is `NEEDS_VERIFICATION` with a note to re-run QA after CI completes.
   666→
   667→---
   668→
   669→### Quality Checks (Multi-Agent) — REQUIRED
   670→
   671→**You MUST spawn sub-agents for quality checks.** Do NOT run these checks inline with bash commands. Sub-agents provide parallel execution, better context isolation, and consistent reporting.
   672→
   673→**Execution mode:** Respect the agent execution mode determined above (see "Agent Execution Mode" section).
   674→
   675→#### If parallel mode enabled:
   676→
   677→**Spawn ALL THREE agents in a SINGLE message (one Tool call per agent, all in same response):**
   678→
   679→1. `Task(subagent_type="general-purpose", model="haiku", prompt="Run type safety and deleted tests checks on the current branch vs main. Report: type issues count, deleted tests, verdict.")`
   680→
   681→2. `Task(subagent_type="general-purpose", model="haiku", prompt="Run scope and size checks on the current branch vs main. Report: files count, diff size, size assessment.")`
   682→
   683→3. `Task(subagent_type="general-purpose", model="haiku", prompt="Run security scan on changed files in current branch vs main. Report: critical/warning/info counts, verdict.")`
   684→
   685→#### If sequential mode (default):
   686→
   687→**Spawn each agent ONE AT A TIME, waiting for each to complete before the next:**
   688→
   689→1. **First:** `Task(subagent_type="general-purpose", model="haiku", prompt="Run type safety and deleted tests checks on the current branch vs main. Report: type issues count, deleted tests, verdict.")`
   690→
   691→2. **After #1 completes:** `Task(subagent_type="general-purpose", model="haiku", prompt="Run scope and size checks on the current branch vs main. Report: files count, diff size, size assessment.")`
   692→
   693→3. **After #2 completes:** `Task(subagent_type="general-purpose", model="haiku", prompt="Run security scan on changed files in current branch vs main. Report: critical/warning/info counts, verdict.")`
   694→
   695→**Add RLS check if admin files modified:**
   696→```bash
   697→admin_modified=$(git diff main...HEAD --name-only | grep -E "^app/admin/" | head -1 || true)
   698→```
   699→
   700→See [quality-gates.md](references/quality-gates.md) for detailed verdict synthesis.
   701→
   702→### Using MCP Tools (Optional)
   703→
   704→- **Sequential Thinking:** For complex multi-step analysis
   705→- **Context7:** For broader pattern context and library documentation
   706→
   707→### 1. Context and AC Alignment
   708→
   709→- **Read all GitHub issue comments** for complete context
   710→- Reconstruct the AC checklist (AC-1, AC-2, ...)
   711→- If AC unclear, state assumptions explicitly
   712→
   713→### 2. Code Review
   714→
   715→Perform a code review focusing on:
   716→
   717→- Correctness and potential bugs
   718→- Readability and maintainability
   719→- Alignment with existing patterns (see CLAUDE.md)
   720→- TypeScript strictness and type safety
   721→- **Duplicate utility check:** Verify new utilities don't duplicate existing ones in `docs/patterns/`
   722→
   723→See [code-review-checklist.md](references/code-review-checklist.md) for integration verification steps.
   724→
   725→### 2a. Build Verification (When Build Fails)
   726→
   727→**When to apply:** `npm run build` fails on the feature branch.
   728→
   729→**Purpose:** Distinguish between pre-existing build failures (already on main) and regressions introduced by this PR.
   730→
   731→**Detection:**
   732→```bash
   733→# Run build and capture result
   734→npm run build 2>&1
   735→BUILD_EXIT_CODE=$?
   736→```
   737→
   738→**If build fails, verify against main:**
   739→
   740→The quality-checks.sh script includes `run_build_with_verification()` which:
   741→1. Runs `npm run build` on the feature branch
   742→2. If it fails, runs build on main branch (via the main repo directory)
   743→3. Compares exit codes and first error lines
   744→4. Produces a "Build Verification" table (see AC-4)
   745→
   746→**Verification Logic:**
   747→
   748→| Feature Build | Main Build | Error Match | Result |
   749→|---------------|------------|-------------|--------|
   750→| ❌ Fail | ✅ Pass | N/A | **Regression** - failure introduced by PR |
   751→| ❌ Fail | ❌ Fail | Same error | **Pre-existing** - not blocking |
   752→| ❌ Fail | ❌ Fail | Different | **Unknown** - manual review needed |
   753→| ✅ Pass | * | N/A | No verification needed |
   754→
   755→**Verdict Impact:**
   756→
   757→| Build Verification Result | Verdict Impact |
   758→|---------------------------|----------------|
   759→| Regression detected | `AC_NOT_MET` - must fix before merge |
   760→| Pre-existing failure | No impact - document and proceed |
   761→| Unknown (different errors) | `AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS` - manual review |
   762→| Build passes | No impact |
   763→
   764→**Output Format:**
   765→
   766→```markdown
   767→### Build Verification
   768→
   769→| Check | Status |
   770→|-------|--------|
   771→| Feature branch build | ❌ Failed |
   772→| Main branch build | ❌ Failed |
   773→| Error match | ✅ Same error |
   774→| Regression | **No** (pre-existing) |
   775→
   776→**Note:** Build failure is pre-existing on main branch. Not blocking this PR.
   777→```
   778→
   779→### 2b. Test Coverage Transparency (REQUIRED)
   780→
   781→**Purpose:** Report which changed files have corresponding tests, not just "N tests passed."
   782→
   783→**After running `npm test`, you MUST analyze test coverage for changed files:**
   784→
   785→Use the Glob tool to check for corresponding test files:
   786→```
   787→# Get changed source files (excluding tests) from git
   788→changed=$(git diff main...HEAD --name-only | grep -E '\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$' | grep -v -E '\.test\.|\.spec\.|__tests__' || true)
   789→
   790→# For each changed file, use the Glob tool to find matching test files
   791→# Glob(pattern="**/${base}.test.*") or Glob(pattern="**/${base}.spec.*")
   792→# If no test file found, report "NO TEST: $file"
   793→```
   794→
   795→**Required reporting format:**
   796→
   797→| Scenario | Report |
   798→|----------|--------|
   799→| Tests cover changed files | `Tests: N passed (covers changed files)` |
   800→| Tests don't cover changed files | `Tests: N passed (⚠️ 0 cover changed files)` |
   801→| No tests for specific files | `Tests: N passed (⚠️ NO TESTS: file1.ts, file2.ts)` |
   802→
   803→**Include in output template:**
   804→
   805→```markdown
   806→### Test Coverage Analysis
   807→
   808→| Changed File | Has Tests? | Test File |
   809→|--------------|------------|-----------|
   810→| `lib/foo.ts` | ✅ Yes | `__tests__/foo.test.ts` |
   811→| `lib/bar.ts` | ⚠️ No | - |
   812→
   813→**Coverage:** X/Y changed files have tests
   814→```
   815→
   816→### 2c. Change Tier Classification
   817→
   818→**Purpose:** Flag coverage gaps based on criticality, not just presence/absence.
   819→
   820→**Tier definitions:**
   821→
   822→| Tier | Change Type | Coverage Requirement |
   823→|------|-------------|---------------------|
   824→| **Critical** | Auth, payments, security, server-actions, middleware, admin | Flag prominently if missing |
   825→| **Standard** | Business logic, API handlers, utilities | Note if missing |
   826→| **Optional** | Config, types-only, UI tweaks | No flag needed |
   827→
   828→**Detection heuristic:**
   829→
   830→```bash
   831→# Detect critical paths in changed files
   832→changed=$(git diff main...HEAD --name-only | grep -E '\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$' || true)
   833→critical=$(echo "$changed" | grep -E 'auth|payment|security|server-action|middleware|admin' || true)
   834→
   835→if [[ -n "$critical" ]]; then
   836→  echo "⚠️ CRITICAL PATH CHANGES (test coverage strongly recommended):"
   837→  echo "$critical"
   838→fi
   839→```
   840→
   841→**Reporting format:**
   842→
   843→```markdown
   844→### Change Tiers
   845→
   846→| Tier | Files | Coverage Status |
   847→|------|-------|-----------------|
   848→| Critical | `auth/login.ts` | ⚠️ NO TESTS - Flag prominently |
   849→| Standard | `lib/utils.ts` | Note: No tests |
   850→| Optional | `types/index.ts` | OK - Types only |
   851→```
   852→
   853→### 2d. Test Quality Review
   854→
   855→**When to apply:** Test files were added or modified.
   856→
   857→Evaluate test quality using the checklist:
   858→- **Behavior vs Implementation:** Tests assert on outputs, not internals
   859→- **Coverage Depth:** Error paths and edge cases covered
   860→- **Mock Hygiene:** Only external dependencies mocked
   861→- **Test Reliability:** No timing dependencies, deterministic
   862→
   863→See [test-quality-checklist.md](references/test-quality-checklist.md) for detailed evaluation criteria.
   864→
   865→**Flag common issues:**
   866→- Over-mocking (4+ modules mocked in single test)
   867→- Missing error path tests
   868→- Snapshot abuse (>50 line snapshots)
   869→- Implementation mirroring
   870→
   871→### 2e. Anti-Pattern Detection
   872→
   873→**Always run** code pattern checks on changed files:
   874→
   875→```bash
   876→# Get changed TypeScript/JavaScript files
   877→changed_files=$(git diff main...HEAD --name-only | grep -E '\.(ts|tsx|js|jsx)$' || true)
   878→```
   879→
   880→**Check for:**
   881→
   882→| Category | Pattern | Risk |
   883→|----------|---------|------|
   884→| Performance | N+1 query (`await` in loop) | ⚠️ Medium |
   885→| Error Handling | Empty catch block | ⚠️ Medium |
   886→| Security | Hardcoded secrets | ❌ High |
   887→| Security | SQL concatenation | ❌ High |
   888→| Memory | Uncleared interval/timeout | ⚠️ Medium |
   889→| A11y | Image without alt | ⚠️ Low |
   890→
   891→**Dependency audit** (when `package.json` modified):
   892→
   893→| Flag | Threshold |
   894→|------|-----------|
   895→| Low downloads | <1,000/week |
   896→| Stale | No updates 12+ months |
   897→| License risk | UNLICENSED, GPL in MIT |
   898→| Security | Known vulnerabilities |
   899→
   900→See [anti-pattern-detection.md](references/anti-pattern-detection.md) for detection commands and full criteria.
   901→
   902→### 2f. Call-Site Review (When New Functions Added)
   903→
   904→**When to apply:** New exported functions are detected in the diff.
   905→
   906→**Purpose:** Review not just the function implementation but **where** and **how** it's called. A function can be perfectly implemented but called incorrectly at the call site. (Origin: Issue #295 — `rebaseBeforePR()` had thorough unit tests but was called for every issue in a chain loop when the AC specified "only the final branch.")
   907→
   908→**Detection:**
   909→```bash
   910→# Find new exported functions (added lines only)
   911→# Catches: export function foo, export async function foo,
   912→#          export const foo = () =>, export const foo = async () =>
   913→fn_exports=$(git diff main...HEAD | grep -E '^\+export (async )?function \w+' | sed 's/^+//' | grep -oE 'function \w+' | awk '{print $2}' || true)
   914→arrow_exports=$(git diff main...HEAD | grep -E '^\+export const \w+ = (async )?\(' | sed 's/^+//' | grep -oE 'const \w+' | awk '{print $2}' || true)
   915→new_exports=$(echo -e "${fn_exports}\n${arrow_exports}" | sed '/^$/d' | sort -u)
   916→export_count=$(echo "$new_exports" | grep -c . || echo 0)
   917→
   918→if [[ $export_count -gt 0 ]]; then
   919→  echo "New exported functions detected: $export_count"
   920→  echo "$new_exports"
   921→fi
   922→```
   923→
   924→**If new exported functions found:**
   925→
   926→#### Step 1: Call-Site Inventory
   927→
   928→For each new exported function, identify ALL call sites using the Grep tool:
   929→
   930→```
   931→# For each new function, find call sites
   932→# Use the Grep tool for each function name:
   933→Grep(pattern="${func}\\(", glob="*.{ts,tsx}", output_mode="content")
   934→# Then exclude test files, __tests__ dirs, and the export definition itself
   935→```
   936→
   937→**Call site types:**
   938→- Direct call: `functionName(args)`
   939→- Method call: `this.functionName(args)` or `obj.functionName(args)`
   940→- Callback: `.then(functionName)` or `array.map(functionName)`
   941→- Conditional: `condition && functionName(args)`
   942→
   943→#### Step 2: Condition Audit
   944→
   945→For each call site, document the conditions that gate the call:
   946→
   947→| Condition Type | Example | Check |
   948→|----------------|---------|-------|
   949→| Guard clause | `if (x) { fn() }` | Does condition match AC? |
   950→| Logical AND | `x && fn()` | Is guard sufficient? |
   951→| Ternary | `x ? fn() : null` | Correct branch? |
   952→| Early return | `if (!x) return; fn()` | Correct logic? |
   953→
   954→**Compare conditions against AC constraints:**
   955→- AC says "only when X" → Call site should have `if (X)` guard
   956→- AC says "not in Y mode" → Call site should have `if (!Y)` guard
   957→- AC says "for Z items" → Call site should filter for Z condition
   958→
   959→#### Step 3: Loop Awareness
   960→
   961→**Detect if function is called inside a loop:**
   962→
   963→```
   964→# For each function, use the Grep tool with context to check surrounding lines:
   965→Grep(pattern="${func}\\(", glob="*.{ts,tsx}", output_mode="content", -B=5)
   966→# Then inspect the context lines for loop constructs:
   967→# for, while, forEach, .map(, .filter(, .reduce(
   968→# If a loop is found, flag: "function called inside loop - verify iteration scope"
   969→```
   970→
   971→**Loop iteration review questions:**
   972→1. Should function run for ALL iterations? → OK if yes
   973→2. Should function run for FIRST/LAST only? → Check for index guard
   974→3. Should function run for SOME iterations? → Check for condition filter
   975→
   976→**Red flags:**
   977→- Function called unconditionally in loop when AC says "only once"
   978→- No break/return after call when AC implies single execution
   979→- Missing mode/flag guard when AC specifies conditions
   980→
   981→#### Step 4: Mode Sensitivity
   982→
   983→If the function accepts configuration or mode options:
   984→- Is the correct mode passed at the call site?
   985→- Are all mode-specific paths exercised appropriately?
   986→
   987→**Output Format:**
   988→
   989→```markdown
   990→### Call-Site Review
   991→
   992→**New exported functions detected:** N
   993→
   994→| Function | Call Sites | Loop? | Conditions | AC Match |
   995→|----------|-----------|-------|------------|----------|
   996→| `newFunction()` | `file.ts:123` | No | `if (success)` | ✅ Matches AC-2 |
   997→| `anotherFunc()` | `run.ts:456` | Yes | None | ⚠️ Missing guard (AC-3 says "final only") |
   998→| `thirdFunc()` | Not called | - | - | ⚠️ Unused export |
   999→
  1000→**Findings:**
  1001→- [List any mismatches between call-site conditions and AC constraints]
  1002→
  1003→**Recommendations:**
  1004→- [Specific fixes needed at call sites]
  1005→```
  1006→
  1007→**Verdict Impact:**
  1008→
  1009→| Finding | Verdict Impact |
  1010→|---------|----------------|
  1011→| All call sites match AC | No impact |
  1012→| Call site missing AC-required guard | `AC_NOT_MET` |
  1013→| Function not called anywhere | `AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS` (dead export) |
  1014→| Call site in loop, AC unclear about iteration | `NEEDS_VERIFICATION` |
  1015→
  1016→See [call-site-review.md](references/call-site-review.md) for detailed methodology and examples.
  1017→
  1018→### 2g. CLI Registration Verification (When Option Interfaces Modified)
  1019→
  1020→**When to apply:** `RunOptions` or similar CLI option interfaces are modified in the diff.
  1021→
  1022→**Purpose:** Detect new option interface fields that have runtime usage (via `mergedOptions.X`) but lack corresponding CLI registration (via `.option()` in `bin/cli.ts`). This class of bug is invisible to TypeScript, build, and unit tests—caught only by manual review or this check.
  1023→
  1024→**Origin:** Issue #305 — `force?: boolean` was added to `RunOptions`, checked at runtime with `mergedOptions.force`, and referenced in user-facing warnings ("use --force to re-run"), but `--force` was never registered in `bin/cli.ts`. The bug passed QA and was caught only by manual cross-reference.
  1025→
  1026→**Detection:**
  1027→
  1028→```bash
  1029→# Check if option interfaces or CLI file were modified
  1030→option_files=$(git diff main...HEAD --name-only | grep -E "batch-executor\.ts|run\.ts|cli\.ts" || true)
  1031→option_modified=$(echo "$option_files" | grep -v "^$" | wc -l | xargs || echo "0")
  1032→
  1033→if [[ $option_modified -gt 0 ]]; then
  1034→  echo "Option interface or CLI file modified - running CLI registration verification"
  1035→fi
  1036→```
  1037→
  1038→**Key File Map:**
  1039→
  1040→| Interface | Location | CLI Registration |
  1041→|-----------|----------|------------------|
  1042→| `RunOptions` | `src/lib/workflow/batch-executor.ts` | `run` command in `bin/cli.ts` |
  1043→
  1044→**Verification Logic:**
  1045→
  1046→1. **Extract new interface fields from diff:**
  1047→   ```bash
  1048→   # Get new fields added to RunOptions (or similar interfaces)
  1049→   new_fields=$(git diff main...HEAD -- src/lib/workflow/batch-executor.ts | \
  1050→     grep -E '^\+\s+\w+\??: ' | \
  1051→     sed 's/.*+ *//' | \
  1052→     sed 's/\?.*//' | \
  1053→     sed 's/:.*//' | \
  1054→     tr -d ' ' || true)
  1055→   ```
  1056→
  1057→2. **Check for runtime usage (mergedOptions.X):**
  1058→   ```bash
  1059→   # For each new field, check if it's used at runtime
  1060→   for field in $new_fields; do
  1061→     runtime_usage=$(git diff main...HEAD | grep -E "mergedOptions\.$field|options\.$field" || true)
  1062→     if [[ -n "$runtime_usage" ]]; then
  1063→       echo "Field '$field' has runtime usage - verify CLI registration"
  1064→     fi
  1065→   done
  1066→   ```
  1067→
  1068→3. **Verify CLI registration exists:**
  1069→   ```bash
  1070→   # Extract registered CLI options from bin/cli.ts
  1071→   # Matches patterns like: --force, --dry-run, --timeout
  1072→   registered=$(grep -oE '"\-\-[a-z-]+"' bin/cli.ts | tr -d '"' | sed 's/^--//' || true)
  1073→
  1074→   # Check if field has corresponding registration
  1075→   # Note: CLI flags use kebab-case, interface fields use camelCase
  1076→   # Example: fieldName → --field-name
  1077→   ```
  1078→
  1079→4. **Internal-only field exclusion (AC-5):**
  1080→
  1081→   Fields without runtime `mergedOptions.X` usage are internal-only and don't need CLI registration:
  1082→   - `autoDetectPhases` — set programmatically, not user-facing
  1083→   - `worktreeIsolation` — environment-controlled
  1084→   - Fields only used in type signatures without runtime access
  1085→
  1086→   **Detection:** If `grep "mergedOptions.$field"` returns no matches, the field is internal-only.
  1087→
  1088→**Output Format:**
  1089→
  1090→```markdown
  1091→### CLI Registration Verification
  1092→
  1093→**Option files modified:** Yes/No
  1094→
  1095→| Interface Field | Runtime Usage | CLI Registered | Status |
  1096→|----------------|--------------|----------------|--------|
  1097→| `force` | `mergedOptions.force` (line 2447) | `--force` in bin/cli.ts | ✅ OK |
  1098→| `newField` | `mergedOptions.newField` (line 500) | NOT REGISTERED | ❌ FAIL |
  1099→| `internalOnly` | None (internal) | N/A | ⏭️ SKIP |
  1100→
  1101→**Verification Status:** Passed / Failed / N/A
  1102→```
  1103→
  1104→**Verdict Gating (AC-4):**
  1105→
  1106→| Verification Status | Maximum Verdict |
  1107→|---------------------|-----------------|
  1108→| Passed | READY_FOR_MERGE |
  1109→| N/A (no option changes) | READY_FOR_MERGE |
  1110→| Failed | AC_NOT_MET |
  1111→
  1112→**CRITICAL:** If CLI registration verification = **Failed**, verdict CANNOT be `READY_FOR_MERGE`. Missing CLI registrations mean users cannot access the feature via command line.
  1113→
  1114→**If verification fails:**
  1115→1. Flag the specific fields missing CLI registration
  1116→2. Set verdict to `AC_NOT_MET`
  1117→3. Include remediation steps:
  1118→   ```markdown
  1119→   **Remediation:**
  1120→   1. Add to `bin/cli.ts` under the appropriate command:
  1121→      ```typescript
  1122→      .option("--field-name", "Description of what the flag does")
  1123→      ```
  1124→   2. Verify with `npx sequant <command> --help`
  1125→   ```
  1126→
  1127→---
  1128→
  1129→### 3. QA vs AC
  1130→
  1131→For each AC item, mark as:
  1132→- `MET`
  1133→- `PARTIALLY_MET`
  1134→- `NOT_MET`
  1135→
  1136→Provide a sentence or two explaining why.
  1137→
  1138→### 3a. AC Status Persistence — REQUIRED
  1139→
  1140→**After evaluating each AC item**, update the status in workflow state using the state CLI:
  1141→
  1142→```bash
  1143→# Step 1: Initialize AC items for the issue (run once, before updating statuses)
  1144→npx tsx scripts/state/update.ts init-ac <issue-number> <ac-count>
  1145→
  1146→# Example: Initialize 4 AC items for issue #250
  1147→npx tsx scripts/state/update.ts init-ac 250 4
  1148→```
  1149→
  1150→```bash
  1151→# Step 2: Update each AC item's status
  1152→npx tsx scripts/state/update.ts ac <issue-number> <ac-id> <status> "<notes>"
  1153→
  1154→# Examples:
  1155→npx tsx scripts/state/update.ts ac 250 AC-1 met "Verified: tests pass and feature works"
  1156→npx tsx scripts/state/update.ts ac 250 AC-2 not_met "Missing error handling for edge case"
  1157→npx tsx scripts/state/update.ts ac 250 AC-3 blocked "Waiting on upstream dependency"
  1158→```
  1159→
  1160→**Status mapping:**
  1161→- `MET` → `met`
  1162→- `PARTIALLY_MET` → `not_met` (with notes explaining what's missing)
  1163→- `NOT_MET` → `not_met`
  1164→- `BLOCKED` → `blocked` (external dependency issue)
  1165→
  1166→**Why this matters:** Updating AC status in state enables:
  1167→- Dashboard shows real-time AC progress per issue
  1168→- Cross-skill tracking of which AC items need work
  1169→- Summary badges show "X/Y met" status
  1170→
  1171→**If issue has no stored AC:**
  1172→- Run `init-ac` first to create the AC items
  1173→- Then update each AC status individually
  1174→
  1175→### 4. Failure Path & Edge Case Testing (REQUIRED)
  1176→
  1177→Before any READY_FOR_MERGE verdict, complete the adversarial thinking checklist:
  1178→
  1179→1. **"What would break this?"** - Identify and test at least 2 failure scenarios
  1180→2. **"What assumptions am I making?"** - List and validate key assumptions
  1181→3. **"What's the unhappy path?"** - Test invalid inputs, failed dependencies
  1182→4. **"Did I test the feature's PRIMARY PURPOSE?"** - If it handles errors, trigger an error
  1183→
  1184→See [testing-requirements.md](references/testing-requirements.md) for edge case checklists.
  1185→
  1186→### 5. Adversarial Self-Evaluation (REQUIRED)
  1187→
  1188→**Before issuing your verdict**, you MUST complete this adversarial self-evaluation to catch issues that automated quality checks miss.
  1189→
  1190→**Why this matters:** QA automation catches type issues, deleted tests, and scope creep - but misses:
  1191→- Features that don't actually work as expected
  1192→- Tests that pass but don't test the right things
  1193→- Edge cases only apparent when actually using the feature
  1194→
  1195→**Answer these questions honestly:**
  1196→1. "Did the implementation actually work when I reviewed it, or am I assuming it works?"
  1197→2. "Do the tests actually test the feature's primary purpose, or just pass?"
  1198→3. "What's the most likely way this feature could break in production?"
  1199→4. "Am I giving a positive verdict because the code looks clean, or because I verified it works?"
  1200→
  1201→**Include this section in your output:**
  1202→
  1203→```markdown
  1204→### Self-Evaluation
  1205→
  1206→- **Verified working:** [Yes/No - did you actually verify the feature works, or assume it does?]
  1207→- **Test efficacy:** [High/Medium/Low - do tests catch the feature breaking?]
  1208→- **Likely failure mode:** [What would most likely break this in production?]
  1209→- **Verdict confidence:** [High/Medium/Low - explain any uncertainty]
  1210→```
  1211→
  1212→**If any answer reveals concerns:**
  1213→- Factor the concerns into your verdict
  1214→- If significant, change verdict to `AC_NOT_MET` or `AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS`
  1215→- Document the concerns in the QA comment
  1216→
  1217→**Do NOT skip this self-evaluation.** Honest reflection catches issues that code review cannot.
  1218→
  1219→#### Skill Change Review (Conditional)
  1220→
  1221→**When to apply:** `.claude/skills/**/*.md` files were modified.
  1222→
  1223→**Detect skill changes:**
  1224→```bash
  1225→skills_changed=$(git diff main...HEAD --name-only | grep -E "^\.claude/skills/.*\.md$" | wc -l | xargs || true)
  1226→```
  1227→
  1228→**If skills_changed > 0, add these adversarial prompts:**
  1229→
  1230→| Prompt | Why It Matters |
  1231→|--------|----------------|
  1232→| **Command verified:** Did you execute at least one referenced command? | Skill instructions can reference commands that don't work (wrong flags, missing fields) |
  1233→| **Fields verified:** For JSON commands, do field names match actual output? | Issue #178: `gh pr checks --json conclusion` failed because `conclusion` doesn't exist |
  1234→| **Patterns complete:** What variations might users write that aren't covered? | Skills define patterns - missing coverage causes silent failures |
  1235→| **Dependencies explicit:** What CLIs/tools does this skill assume are installed? | Missing `gh`, `npm`, etc. breaks the skill with confusing errors |
  1236→
  1237→**Example skill-specific self-evaluation:**
  1238→
  1239→```markdown
  1240→### Skill Change Review
  1241→
  1242→- [ ] **Command verified:** Executed `gh pr checks --json name,state,bucket` - fields exist ✅
  1243→- [ ] **Fields verified:** Checked `gh pr checks --help` for valid JSON fields ✅
  1244→- [ ] **Patterns complete:** Covered SUCCESS, FAILURE, PENDING states ✅
  1245→- [ ] **Dependencies explicit:** Requires `gh` CLI authenticated ✅
  1246→```
  1247→
  1248→---
  1249→
  1250→### 6. Execution Evidence (REQUIRED for scripts/CLI)
  1251→
  1252→**When to apply:** `scripts/` or CLI files were modified.
  1253→
  1254→**Detect change type:**
  1255→```bash
  1256→scripts_changed=$(git diff main...HEAD --name-only | grep -E "^scripts/" | wc -l | xargs || true)
  1257→cli_changed=$(git diff main...HEAD --name-only | grep -E "(cli|commands?)" | wc -l | xargs || true)
  1258→```
  1259→
  1260→**If scripts/CLI changed, execute at least one smoke command:**
  1261→
  1262→| Change Type | Required Command |
  1263→|-------------|------------------|
  1264→| `scripts/` | `npx tsx scripts/<file>.ts --help` |
  1265→| CLI commands | `npx sequant <cmd> --help` or `--dry-run` |
  1266→| Tests only | `npm test -- --grep "feature"` |
  1267→| Types/config only | Waiver with reason |
  1268→
  1269→**Capture evidence:**
  1270→```bash
  1271→# Execute and capture
  1272→npx tsx scripts/example.ts --help 2>&1
  1273→echo "Exit code: $?"
  1274→```
  1275→
  1276→**Evidence status:**
  1277→- **Complete:** All required commands executed successfully
  1278→- **Incomplete:** Some commands not run or failed
  1279→- **Waived:** Explicit reason documented (types-only, config-only)
  1280→- **Not Required:** No executable changes
  1281→
  1282→**Verdict gating:**
  1283→- `READY_FOR_MERGE` requires evidence status: Complete, Waived, or Not Required
  1284→- `AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS` if evidence is Incomplete
  1285→
  1286→See [quality-gates.md](references/quality-gates.md) for detailed evidence requirements.
  1287→
  1288→---
  1289→
  1290→### 6a. Skill Command Verification (REQUIRED for skill changes)
  1291→
  1292→**When to apply:** `.claude/skills/**/*.md` files were modified.
  1293→
  1294→**Purpose:** Skills contain instructions with CLI commands. If those commands have wrong syntax, missing flags, or non-existent JSON fields, the skill will fail when used. QA must verify commands actually work before READY_FOR_MERGE.
  1295→
  1296→**Detect skill changes:**
  1297→```bash
  1298→skills_changed=$(git diff main...HEAD --name-only | grep -E "^\.claude/skills/.*\.md$" || true)
  1299→skill_count=$(echo "$skills_changed" | grep -c . || echo 0)
  1300→```
  1301→
  1302→**Pre-requisite check:**
  1303→```bash
  1304→# Verify gh CLI is available before running verification
  1305→if ! command -v gh &>/dev/null; then
  1306→  echo "⚠️ gh CLI not installed - skill command verification skipped"
  1307→  echo "Install: https://cli.github.com/"
  1308→  # Set verification status to "Skipped" with reason
  1309→fi
  1310→```
  1311→
  1312→**If skill_count > 0, extract and verify commands:**
  1313→
  1314→#### Step 1: Extract Commands from Changed Skills
  1315→
  1316→```bash
  1317→# Extract command patterns from skill files
  1318→for skill_file in $skills_changed; do
  1319→  echo "=== Commands in $skill_file ==="
  1320→
  1321→  # Commands at start of line (simple commands)
  1322→  grep -E '^\s*(gh|npm|npx|git)\s+' "$skill_file" 2>/dev/null | head -10
  1323→
  1324→  # Commands in subshells/variable assignments: result=$(gh pr view ...)
  1325→  grep -oE '\$\((gh|npm|npx|git)\s+[^)]+\)' "$skill_file" 2>/dev/null | head -10
  1326→
  1327→  # Commands in inline backticks
  1328→  grep -oE '\`(gh|npm|npx|git)\s+[^\`]+\`' "$skill_file" 2>/dev/null | head -10
  1329→
  1330→  # Commands after pipe or semicolon: ... | gh ... or ; npm ...
  1331→  grep -oE '[|;]\s*(gh|npm|npx|git)\s+[^|;&]+' "$skill_file" 2>/dev/null | head -10
  1332→done
  1333→```
  1334→
  1335→**Note:** Multi-line commands (using `\` continuation) require manual review. The extraction patterns above capture single-line commands only.
  1336→
  1337→#### Step 2: Verify Command Syntax
  1338→
  1339→For each extracted command type:
  1340→
  1341→| Command Type | Verification Method | Example |
  1342→|--------------|---------------------|---------|
  1343→| `gh pr checks --json X` | Check `gh pr checks --help` for valid JSON fields | `gh pr checks --help \| grep -A 30 "JSON FIELDS"` |
  1344→| `gh issue view --json X` | Check `gh issue view --help` for valid JSON fields | `gh issue view --help \| grep -A 30 "JSON FIELDS"` |
  1345→| `gh api ...` | Verify endpoint format matches GitHub API | Check endpoint structure |
  1346→| `npm run <script>` | Verify script exists in package.json | `jq '.scripts["<script>"]' package.json` |
  1347→| `npx tsx <file>` | Verify file exists | `test -f <file>` |
  1348→| `git <cmd>` | Verify against `git <cmd> --help` | Check valid flags |
  1349→
  1350→**JSON Field Validation Example:**
  1351→
  1352→```bash
  1353→# For commands like: gh pr checks --json name,state,conclusion
  1354→# Verify each field exists
  1355→
  1356→# Get valid fields
  1357→valid_fields=$(gh pr checks --help 2>/dev/null | grep -A 50 "JSON FIELDS" | grep -E "^\s+\w+" | awk '{print $1}' || true)
  1358→
  1359→# Check if "conclusion" is valid (spoiler: it's not)
  1360→echo "$valid_fields" | grep -qw "conclusion" && echo "✅ conclusion exists" || echo "❌ conclusion NOT a valid field"
  1361→```
  1362→
  1363→#### Step 3: Handle Placeholders
  1364→
  1365→Commands with placeholders (`<issue-number>`, `$PR_NUMBER`, `${VAR}`) cannot be executed directly.
  1366→
  1367→**Handling:**
  1368→- **Skip execution** for commands with placeholders
  1369→- **Mark as "Syntax verified, execution skipped"**
  1370→- **Still verify JSON fields** by extracting field names
  1371→
  1372→```bash
  1373→# Example: gh pr checks $pr_number --json name,state,bucket
  1374→# Can't execute (no $pr_number), but can verify fields
  1375→echo "name,state,bucket" | tr ',' '\n' | while read field; do
  1376→  gh pr checks --help | grep -qw "$field" && echo "✅ $field" || echo "❌ $field"
  1377→done
  1378→```
  1379→
  1380→#### Step 4: Command Verification Status
  1381→
  1382→| Status | Meaning |
  1383→|--------|---------|
  1384→| **Passed** | All commands verified, fields exist |
  1385→| **Failed** | At least one command has invalid syntax or non-existent fields |
  1386→| **Skipped** | Commands have placeholders; syntax looks valid but not executed |
  1387→| **Not Required** | No skill files changed |
  1388→
  1389→#### Verdict Gating
  1390→
  1391→**CRITICAL:** If skill command verification = **Failed**, verdict CANNOT be `READY_FOR_MERGE`.
  1392→
  1393→| Verification Status | Maximum Verdict |
  1394→|---------------------|-----------------|
  1395→| Passed | READY_FOR_MERGE |
  1396→| Skipped | READY_FOR_MERGE (with note about unverified placeholders) |
  1397→| Failed | AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS (blocks merge until fixed) |
  1398→| Not Required | READY_FOR_MERGE |
  1399→
  1400→**Output Format:**
  1401→
  1402→```markdown
  1403→### Skill Command Verification
  1404→
  1405→**Skill files changed:** 2
  1406→
  1407→| File | Commands Found | Verification Status |
  1408→|------|----------------|---------------------|
  1409→| `.claude/skills/qa/SKILL.md` | 5 | ✅ Passed |
  1410→| `.claude/skills/exec/SKILL.md` | 3 | ⚠️ Skipped (placeholders) |
  1411→
  1412→**Commands Verified:**
  1413→- `gh pr checks --json name,state,bucket` → ✅ All fields exist
  1414→- `gh issue view --json title,body` → ✅ All fields exist
  1415→
  1416→**Commands with Issues:**
  1417→- `gh pr checks --json conclusion` → ❌ Field "conclusion" does not exist
  1418→
  1419→**Verification Status:** Failed
  1420→```
  1421→
  1422→---
  1423→
  1424→### 6b. Smoke Test (CONDITIONAL)
  1425→
  1426→**When to apply:** Feature changes workflow behavior (skills, CLI commands, scripts).
  1427→
  1428→**Detection:**
  1429→```bash
  1430→# Detect workflow-affecting changes
  1431→skills_changed=$(git diff main...HEAD --name-only | grep -E "^\.claude/skills/" | wc -l | xargs || true)
  1432→scripts_changed=$(git diff main...HEAD --name-only | grep -E "^scripts/" | wc -l | xargs || true)
  1433→cli_changed=$(git diff main...HEAD --name-only | grep -E "^(src/cli|bin)/" | wc -l | xargs || true)
  1434→
  1435→if [[ $((skills_changed + scripts_changed + cli_changed)) -gt 0 ]]; then
  1436→  echo "Smoke test recommended for workflow changes"
  1437→fi
  1438→```
  1439→
  1440→**Smoke Test Checklist:**
  1441→1. **Happy path:** Execute the primary use case
  1442→2. **Edge cases:** Test graceful handling (missing deps, invalid input)
  1443→3. **Error detection:** Verify errors are caught and reported
  1444→
  1445→**Output Format:**
  1446→
  1447→| Test | Command | Result | Notes |
  1448→|------|---------|--------|-------|
  1449→| Happy path | `[command]` | ✅/❌ | [observation] |
  1450→| Edge case | `[command]` | ✅/❌ | [observation] |
  1451→| Error handling | `[command]` | ✅/❌ | [observation] |
  1452→
  1453→**Smoke Test Status:**
  1454→- **Complete:** All applicable tests passed
  1455→- **Partial:** Some tests skipped or failed (document why)
  1456→- **Not Required:** No workflow-affecting changes
  1457→
  1458→**Verdict Impact:**
  1459→
  1460→| Smoke Test Status | Verdict Impact |
  1461→|-------------------|----------------|
  1462→| Complete | No impact (positive signal) |
  1463→| Partial | → `AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS` (document gaps) |
  1464→| Not Required | No impact |
  1465→
  1466→---
  1467→
  1468→### 7. A+ Status Verdict
  1469→
  1470→Provide an overall verdict:
  1471→
  1472→- `READY_FOR_MERGE` — ALL ACs are `MET` and code quality is high ("A+")
  1473→- `AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS` — ALL ACs are `MET`, but meaningful improvements recommended
  1474→- `NEEDS_VERIFICATION` — ALL ACs are `MET` or `PENDING`, at least one requires external verification
  1475→- `AC_NOT_MET` — One or more ACs are `NOT_MET` or `PARTIALLY_MET`
  1476→
  1477→**Verdict Determination Algorithm (REQUIRED):**
  1478→
  1479→```text
  1480→1. Count AC statuses (INCLUDES both original AND derived ACs):
  1481→   - met_count = ACs with status MET (original + derived)
  1482→   - partial_count = ACs with status PARTIALLY_MET (original + derived)
  1483→   - pending_count = ACs with status PENDING (original + derived)
  1484→   - not_met_count = ACs with status NOT_MET (original + derived)
  1485→
  1486→   NOTE: Derived ACs are treated IDENTICALLY to original ACs.
  1487→   A derived AC marked NOT_MET will block merge just like an original AC.
  1488→
  1489→2. Check verification gates:
  1490→   - skill_verification = status from Section 6a (Passed/Failed/Skipped/Not Required)
  1491→   - execution_evidence = status from Section 6 (Complete/Incomplete/Waived/Not Required)
  1492→   - quality_plan_status = status from Phase 0b (Complete/Partial/Not Addressed/N/A)
  1493→   - smoke_test_status = status from Section 6b (Complete/Partial/Not Required)
  1494→
  1495→3. Browser testing enforcement check:
  1496→   - Check if any .tsx files were changed: git diff main...HEAD --name-only | grep '\.tsx$' || true
  1497→   - Check if /test phase ran: look for test phase marker in issue comments
  1498→   - Check if issue has 'no-browser-test' label
  1499→   - IF .tsx files changed AND /test did NOT run AND no 'no-browser-test' label:
  1500→       → Set browser_test_missing = true
  1501→
  1502→4. Determine verdict (in order):
  1503→   - IF not_met_count > 0 OR partial_count > 0:
  1504→       → AC_NOT_MET (block merge)
  1505→   - ELSE IF skill_verification == "Failed":
  1506→       → AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS (skill commands have issues - cannot be READY_FOR_MERGE)
  1507→   - ELSE IF execution_evidence == "Incomplete":
  1508→       → AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS (scripts not verified - cannot be READY_FOR_MERGE)
  1509→   - ELSE IF quality_plan_status == "Not Addressed" AND quality_plan_exists:
  1510→       → AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS (quality dimensions not addressed - flag for review)
  1511→   - ELSE IF browser_test_missing (from step 3):
  1512→       → AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS (browser testing recommended for .tsx changes)
  1513→         Note: "Browser testing recommended: .tsx files modified without /test phase.
  1514→               Add 'ui' label to enable, or 'no-browser-test' to opt out."
  1515→   - ELSE IF pending_count > 0:
  1516→       → NEEDS_VERIFICATION (wait for verification)
  1517→   - ELSE IF quality_plan_status == "Partial":
  1518→       → AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS (some quality dimensions incomplete - can merge with notes)
  1519→   - ELSE IF smoke_test_status == "Partial":
  1520→       → AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS (smoke tests incomplete - document gaps before merge)
  1521→   - ELSE IF improvement_suggestions.length > 0:
  1522→       → AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS (can merge with notes)
  1523→   - ELSE:
  1524→       → READY_FOR_MERGE (A+ implementation)
  1525→```
  1526→
  1527→**Browser Testing Enforcement:**
  1528→
  1529→Before finalizing the verdict, check for missing browser test coverage:
  1530→
  1531→```bash
  1532→# Check if .tsx files were changed
  1533→tsx_changed=$(git diff main...HEAD --name-only | grep '\.tsx$' || true)
  1534→
  1535→# Check if /test phase ran (look for test phase marker in issue comments)
  1536→test_ran=$(gh issue view <issue-number> --json comments --jq '[.comments[].body]' | \
  1537→  grep -o '{"phase":"test"' || true)
  1538→
  1539→# Check for no-browser-test label
  1540→no_browser_test=$(gh issue view <issue-number> --json labels --jq '.labels[].name' | \
  1541→  grep 'no-browser-test' || true)
  1542→
  1543→if [[ -n "$tsx_changed" && -z "$test_ran" && -z "$no_browser_test" ]]; then
  1544→  echo "⚠️ Browser testing recommended: .tsx files modified without /test phase"
  1545→  # Force verdict to AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS (cannot be READY_FOR_MERGE)
  1546→fi
  1547→```
  1548→
  1549→| Condition | Verdict Effect |
  1550→|-----------|---------------|
  1551→| `.tsx` changed + `/test` ran | Normal verdict |
  1552→| `.tsx` changed + `no-browser-test` label | Normal verdict (explicit opt-out) |
  1553→| `.tsx` changed + no `/test` + no opt-out | Force `AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS` |
  1554→| No `.tsx` changed | Normal verdict |
  1555→
  1556→**CRITICAL:** `PARTIALLY_MET` is NOT sufficient for merge. It MUST be treated as `NOT_MET` for verdict purposes.
  1557→
  1558→**CRITICAL:** If skill command verification = "Failed", verdict CANNOT be `READY_FOR_MERGE`. This prevents shipping skills with broken commands (like issue #178's `conclusion` field).
  1559→
  1560→See [quality-gates.md](references/quality-gates.md) for detailed verdict criteria.
  1561→
  1562→---
  1563→
  1564→## Automated Quality Checks (Reference)
  1565→
  1566→**Note:** These commands are what the sub-agents execute internally. You do NOT run these directly — the sub-agents spawned above handle this. This section is reference documentation only.
  1567→
  1568→```bash
  1569→# Type safety
  1570→type_issues=$(git diff main...HEAD | grep -E ":\s*any[,)]|as any" | wc -l | xargs || true)
  1571→
  1572→# Deleted tests
  1573→deleted_tests=$(git diff main...HEAD --diff-filter=D --name-only | grep -E "\\.test\\.|\\spec\\." | wc -l | xargs || true)
  1574→
  1575→# Scope check
  1576→files_changed=$(git diff main...HEAD --name-only | wc -l | xargs)
  1577→
  1578→# Size check
  1579→additions=$(git diff main...HEAD --numstat | awk '{sum+=$1} END {print sum+0}')
  1580→deletions=$(git diff main...HEAD --numstat | awk '{sum+=$2} END {print sum+0}')
  1581→
  1582→# Security scan
  1583→npx tsx scripts/lib/__tests__/run-security-scan.ts 2>/dev/null
  1584→```
  1585→
  1586→See [scripts/quality-checks.sh](scripts/quality-checks.sh) for the complete automation script.
  1587→
  1588→### 8. Draft Review/QA Comment
  1589→
  1590→Produce a Markdown snippet for the PR/issue:
  1591→- Short summary of the change
  1592→- AC coverage status (bullet list)
  1593→- Key strengths and issues
  1594→- Clear, actionable next steps
  1595→
  1596→### 9. Update GitHub Issue
  1597→
  1598→**If orchestrated (SEQUANT_ORCHESTRATOR is set):**
  1599→- Skip posting GitHub comment (orchestrator handles aggregated summary)
  1600→- Include verdict and AC coverage in output for orchestrator to capture
  1601→- Let orchestrator update labels based on final workflow status
  1602→
  1603→**If standalone:**
  1604→
  1605→Post the draft comment to GitHub and update labels:
  1606→
  1607→- `AC_NOT_MET`: add `needs-work` label
  1608→- `READY_FOR_MERGE`: add `ready-for-review` label
  1609→- `AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS`: add `needs-improvement` label
  1610→- `NEEDS_VERIFICATION`: add `needs-verification` label
  1611→
  1612→### 10. Documentation Reminder
  1613→
  1614→If verdict is `READY_FOR_MERGE` or `AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS`:
  1615→
  1616→```md
  1617→**Documentation:** Before merging, run `/docs <issue>` to generate feature documentation.
  1618→```
  1619→
  1620→### 10a. CHANGELOG Quality Gate (REQUIRED)
  1621→
  1622→**Purpose:** Verify user-facing changes have corresponding CHANGELOG entries before `READY_FOR_MERGE`.
  1623→
  1624→**Detection:**
  1625→
  1626→```bash
  1627→# Check if CHANGELOG.md exists
  1628→if [ ! -f "CHANGELOG.md" ]; then
  1629→  echo "No CHANGELOG.md found - skip CHANGELOG check"
  1630→  exit 0
  1631→fi
  1632→
  1633→# Check if [Unreleased] section has entries
  1634→unreleased_entries=$(sed -n '/^## \[Unreleased\]/,/^## \[/p' CHANGELOG.md | grep -E '^\s*-' | wc -l | xargs || true)
  1635→
  1636→# Determine if change is user-facing (new features, bug fixes, etc.)
  1637→# Look at commit messages or file changes
  1638→user_facing=$(git log main..HEAD --oneline | grep -iE '^[a-f0-9]+ (feat|fix|perf|refactor|docs):' | wc -l | xargs || true)
  1639→```
  1640→
  1641→**Verification Logic:**
  1642→
  1643→| Condition | CHANGELOG Entry Required? | Action |
  1644→|-----------|---------------------------|--------|
  1645→| User-facing changes detected + CHANGELOG exists | ✅ Yes | Check for `[Unreleased]` entry |
  1646→| User-facing changes + no entry | ⚠️ Block | Flag as missing CHANGELOG |
  1647→| Non-user-facing changes (test, ci, chore) | ❌ No | Skip check |
  1648→| No CHANGELOG.md in repo | ❌ No | Skip check |
  1649→
  1650→**If CHANGELOG entry is missing:**
  1651→
  1652→1. Do NOT give `READY_FOR_MERGE` verdict
  1653→2. Set verdict to `AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS` with note:
  1654→   ```markdown
  1655→   **CHANGELOG:** Missing entry for user-facing changes. Add entry to `## [Unreleased]` section before merging.
  1656→   ```
  1657→3. Include this in the draft review comment
  1658→
  1659→**CHANGELOG Entry Validation:**
  1660→
  1661→When an entry exists, verify it follows the format:
  1662→- Starts with action verb (Add, Fix, Update, Remove, Improve)
  1663→- Includes issue number `(#123)`
  1664→- Is under the correct section (Added, Fixed, Changed, etc.)
  1665→
  1666→**Example validation:**
  1667→
  1668→```markdown
  1669→### CHANGELOG Verification
  1670→
  1671→| Check | Status |
  1672→|-------|--------|
  1673→| CHANGELOG.md exists | ✅ Found |
  1674→| User-facing changes | ✅ Yes (feat: commit detected) |
  1675→| [Unreleased] entry | ✅ Present |
  1676→| Entry format | ✅ Valid (includes issue number) |
  1677→
  1678→**Result:** CHANGELOG requirements met
  1679→```
  1680→
  1681→**If CHANGELOG is not required:**
  1682→
  1683→```markdown
  1684→### CHANGELOG Verification
  1685→
  1686→**Result:** N/A (non-user-facing changes only)
  1687→```
  1688→
  1689→---
  1690→
  1691→### 11. Script/CLI Execution Verification
  1692→
  1693→**REQUIRED for CLI/script features:** When `scripts/` files are modified, execution verification is required before `READY_FOR_MERGE`.
  1694→
  1695→**Detection:**
  1696→```bash
  1697→scripts_changed=$(git diff main...HEAD --name-only | grep -E "^(scripts/|templates/scripts/)" | wc -l | xargs || true)
  1698→if [[ $scripts_changed -gt 0 ]]; then
  1699→  echo "Script changes detected. Run /verify before READY_FOR_MERGE"
  1700→fi
  1701→```
  1702→
  1703→**Verification evidence:** Look for "Execution Verification" section in issue comments. This section is posted by the `/verify` skill and includes:
  1704→- Command executed
  1705→- Exit code and duration
  1706→- Output sample (truncated)
  1707→- Human confirmation of expected behavior
  1708→
  1709→**If no verification evidence exists:**
  1710→1. Prompt: "Script changes detected but no execution verification found. Run `/verify <issue> --command \"<test command>\"` before READY_FOR_MERGE verdict."
  1711→2. Do NOT give `READY_FOR_MERGE` verdict until verification is complete (unless an approved override applies — see Section 11a)
  1712→3. Verdict should be `AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS` with note about missing verification
  1713→
  1714→**Why this matters:**
  1715→- Code review + unit tests miss integration issues
  1716→- CLI features must be executed at least once to verify end-to-end behavior
  1717→
  1718→**Example workflow:**
  1719→```bash
  1720→# QA detects scripts/ or templates/scripts/ changes
  1721→# -> Prompt: "Run /verify before READY_FOR_MERGE"
  1722→
  1723→/verify 558 --command "npx tsx scripts/migrate.ts --dry-run"
  1724→
  1725→# Human confirms output looks correct
  1726→# -> /verify posts evidence to issue
  1727→
  1728→/qa 558  # Re-run, now sees verification, can give READY_FOR_MERGE
  1729→```
  1730→
  1731→### 11a. Script Verification Override
  1732→
  1733→In some cases, `/verify` execution can be safely skipped when script changes are purely cosmetic or have no runtime impact. **Overrides require explicit justification and risk assessment.**
  1734→
  1735→**Override Format (REQUIRED when skipping /verify):**
  1736→
  1737→```markdown
  1738→### Script Verification Override
  1739→
  1740→**Requirement:** `/verify` before READY_FOR_MERGE
  1741→**Override:** Yes
  1742→**Justification:** [One of the approved categories below]
  1743→**Risk Assessment:** [None/Low/Medium]
  1744→```
  1745→
  1746→**Approved Override Categories:**
  1747→
  1748→| Category | Example | Risk |
  1749→|----------|---------|------|
  1750→| Syntax-only refactor | `catch (error)` → `catch` | None |
  1751→| Comment/documentation changes | Adding JSDoc, updating comments | None |
  1752→| Type annotation additions | Adding `: string`, `: number` | None |
  1753→| Import reorganization | Sorting imports, removing unused | None |
  1754→| Variable rename (no logic change) | `foo` → `bar` with no behavioral change | Low |
  1755→| Dead code removal | Removing unreachable branches | Low |
  1756→
  1757→**NOT Approved for Override (always require /verify):**
  1758→
  1759→| Category | Example | Why |
  1760→|----------|---------|-----|
  1761→| Logic changes | Modified conditionals, new branches | Runtime behavior changes |
  1762→| New functionality | Added functions, new exports | Must verify execution |
  1763→| Dependency changes | Updated imports from new packages | May affect runtime |
  1764→| Error handling changes | Modified catch blocks, new try/catch | Failure paths change |
  1765→| Configuration changes | Modified env vars, config parsing | Environment-dependent |
  1766→
  1767→**Risk Assessment Definitions:**
  1768→
  1769→| Level | Meaning | Criteria |
  1770→|-------|---------|----------|
  1771→| **None** | Zero runtime impact | Change is invisible at runtime (comments, types, syntax) |
  1772→| **Low** | Negligible runtime impact | Change is cosmetic (rename, dead code) with no logical effect |
  1773→| **Medium** | Possible runtime impact | Change touches executable code but appears safe — **should NOT be overridden** |
  1774→
  1775→**Override Decision Flow:**
  1776→
  1777→1. Check if change matches an approved category → If no, `/verify` is required
  1778→2. Assess risk level → If Medium or higher, `/verify` is required
  1779→3. Document override using the format above in the QA output
  1780→4. Include override in the GitHub issue comment for audit trail
  1781→
  1782→**CRITICAL:** When in doubt, run `/verify`. Overrides are for clear-cut cases only. If you need to argue that a change is safe, it probably needs verification.
  1783→
  1784→---
  1785→
  1786→## State Tracking
  1787→
  1788→**IMPORTANT:** Update workflow state when running standalone (not orchestrated).
  1789→
  1790→### State Updates (Standalone Only)
  1791→
  1792→When NOT orchestrated (`SEQUANT_ORCHESTRATOR` is not set):
  1793→
  1794→**At skill start:**
  1795→```bash
  1796→npx tsx scripts/state/update.ts start <issue-number> qa
  1797→```
  1798→
  1799→**On successful completion (READY_FOR_MERGE or AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS):**
  1800→```bash
  1801→npx tsx scripts/state/update.ts complete <issue-number> qa
  1802→npx tsx scripts/state/update.ts status <issue-number> ready_for_merge
  1803→```
  1804→
  1805→**On failure (AC_NOT_MET):**
  1806→```bash
  1807→npx tsx scripts/state/update.ts fail <issue-number> qa "AC not met"
  1808→```
  1809→
  1810→**Why this matters:** State tracking enables dashboard visibility, resume capability, and workflow orchestration. Skills update state when standalone; orchestrators handle state when running workflows.
  1811→

  1814→## Output Verification
  1815→
  1816→**Before responding, verify your output includes ALL of these:**
  1817→
  1818→### Standard QA (Implementation Exists)
  1819→
  1820→- [ ] **Self-Evaluation Completed** - Adversarial self-evaluation section included in output
  1821→- [ ] **AC Coverage** - Each AC item marked as MET, PARTIALLY_MET, NOT_MET, PENDING, or N/A
  1822→- [ ] **Quality Plan Verification** - Included if quality plan exists (or marked N/A if no quality plan)
  1823→- [ ] **CI Status** - Included if PR exists (or marked "No PR" / "No CI configured")
  1824→- [ ] **Verdict** - One of: READY_FOR_MERGE, AC_MET_BUT_NOT_A_PLUS, NEEDS_VERIFICATION, AC_NOT_MET
  1825→- [ ] **Quality Metrics** - Type issues, deleted tests, files changed, additions/deletions
  1826→- [ ] **Cache Status** - Included if caching enabled (or marked N/A if --no-cache)
  1827→- [ ] **Build Verification** - Included if build failed (or marked N/A if build passed)
  1828→- [ ] **Test Coverage Analysis** - Changed files with/without tests, critical paths flagged
  1829→- [ ] **Code Review Findings** - Strengths, issues, suggestions
  1830→- [ ] **Test Quality Review** - Included if test files modified (or marked N/A)
  1831→- [ ] **Anti-Pattern Detection** - Dependency audit (if package.json changed) + code patterns
  1832→- [ ] **Call-Site Review** - Included if new exported functions detected (or marked N/A)
  1833→- [ ] **Execution Evidence** - Included if scripts/CLI modified (or marked N/A)
  1834→- [ ] **Script Verification Override** - Included if scripts/CLI modified AND /verify was skipped (with justification and risk assessment)
  1835→- [ ] **Skill Command Verification** - Included if `.claude/skills/**/*.md` modified (or marked N/A)
  1836→- [ ] **Skill Change Review** - Skill-specific adversarial prompts included if skills changed
  1837→- [ ] **Smoke Test** - Included if workflow-affecting changes (skills, scripts, CLI), or marked "Not Required"
  1838→- [ ] **CHANGELOG Verification** - User-facing changes have `[Unreleased]` entry (or marked N/A)
  1839→- [ ] **Documentation Check** - README/docs updated if feature adds new functionality
  1840→- [ ] **Next Steps** - Clear, actionable recommendations
  1841→
  1842→### Early Exit (No Implementation)
  1843→
  1844→When early exit is triggered (no commits, no uncommitted changes, no PR):
  1845→
  1846→- [ ] **Implementation Status** - Clearly states "NOT FOUND"
  1847→- [ ] **Verdict** - Must be `AC_NOT_MET`
  1848→- [ ] **Next Steps** - Directs user to run `/exec` first
  1849→- [ ] **Sub-agents NOT spawned** - Quality check agents were skipped
  1850→
  1851→**DO NOT respond until all applicable items are verified.**
